record.title: Walter Johnson, 1974-01-16, Interview - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.origin: Parents were Swedish immigrants who moved from Minnesota (1882)

point.title: Account of the murder of Dr. Watkins by William Steffens
point.synopsis: Account of the murder of Dr. Watkins by William Steffens. Hiding out and pursuing Steffen. Buffalo Coat, a novel, written around this event.

point.title: Names for towns; Russian farmers responsible for naming Moscow; homesteaders
point.synopsis: Indian, French, and Spanish names for towns. Russian farmers responsible for naming of Moscow? Homestead locators responsible for determining boundary murders. Metric system verses our system.

point.title: Gypsies used to camp near Moscow
point.synopsis: Gypsies used to camp near Moscow and made baskets and plant stands out of willows and sell them around town. Wild horses could be bought for 500 apiece. Wild Davey.

point.title: Dernam and Kaulfman store
point.synopsis: Dernam and Kaulfman store. David's carried Groceries until the Farmer's Union came in. David's was a friendly store, farmers would gather around the stoves at lunchtime. Copper vacuum tubes carried cash from the clerks to the accounting office.

record.description: Killing of Dr. Watkins. David's Store. 1-16-74 .5 hr RM
record.transcript: Download the PDF Index No transcript available.
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
